verses.text: If a soul trespass against the LORD — lie to his neighbor in a deposit, robbery, oppression; find a lost thing and lie; swear falsely — restore the principal, add a fifth, on the day of his trespass offering. Bring a ram without blemish to the priest. Priest atones; forgiven. Guidance: Sin against neighbor is sin against the LORD. The trespass offering can't be brought until restitution is made — full plus twenty percent. God restores His altar by first restoring the wronged neighbor.

This is the law of the burnt offering. It burns all night till morning; fire ever burning. The priest puts on linen, takes ashes from the altar, lays beside it. Changes garments, carries ashes outside the camp. Fire shall not go out. Every morning lay wood, the burnt offering, the fat. Fire ever burning, never out. Guidance: The fire on God's altar never dies. The first job each morning is to take out yesterday's ashes and rebuild. Holiness keeps no hours; only attentive hands keep it lit.

Law of the meat offering: Aaron's sons offer it before the altar. Take a handful of flour, oil, all the frankincense — burn as memorial. The remainder they eat, unleavened, in the court. Their portion of the LORD's fire offerings — most holy, like sin and trespass offerings. All male priests eat. What touches it is holy. Guidance: The priests eat in the court, unleavened, holy bread from God's table. Their food is God's food. Even the touch of it makes things holy.

Aaron's offering on the day he is anointed — a tenth of an ephah of flour, half morning, half night. With oil; baked, broken, offered. The anointed priest from his sons offers it — perpetual statute, wholly burnt, not eaten. Guidance: When the priest is anointed, his daily bread goes wholly on the altar. He cannot eat his own ministering portion. Those nearest the altar keep the smallest plate; God Himself becomes their food.

Sin offering killed where the burnt is killed — most holy. The priest eats it in the court. What touches its flesh is holy. Earthen vessel broken; brass scoured. All male priests eat. But no sin offering whose blood was brought inside is eaten — burnt in fire. Guidance: Some sin offerings the priests eat; some are burned outside. The deeper the sin reached, the further the carcass goes. Hebrews 13: Jesus suffered outside the gate as the deepest sin offering.
